text: Figure 15.8 Wasserstein or earth mover’s distance. — Labels: c), d)

Figure 15.8 Wasserstein or earth mover’s distance. a) Consider the discrete distribution $Pr(x = i)$ . b) We wish to move the probability mass to create the target distribution $q(x = j)$ . c) The transport plan P identifies how much mass will be moved from i
primal formdual formminimize such that andcTp, Ap = b p ≥ 0maximize such that and

where p contains the vectorized elements $P_{ij}$ that determine the amount of mass moved, c contains the distances, $\mathbf{A}\mathbf{p} = \mathbf{b}$ contains the initial distribution constraints, and $\mathbf{p} \geq 0$ ensures the masses moved are non-neg
[^1]: The mathematical background is omitted due to space constraints. Linear programming is a standard problem with well-known algorithms for finding the minimum.
